regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified

trial_data.trial_name: Hellenic Cooperative Oncology Group phase II study

regimens.notes: Well tolerated with ORR of 38.7% in a phase II study. Preferred regimen for adenocarcinoma of unknown primary [1].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.notes: Weekly schedule may be better tolerated for community-based clinics [2].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.notes: Active regimen with median OS of 11 months in GEFCAPI 02 trial. Superior to cisplatin alone [3].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Capecitabine
drugs.schedule: Twice daily Days 1–14, repeat every 3 weeks

regimens.notes: Active and well-tolerated for presumed GI primary adenocarcinoma [4].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)
regimens.setting: First-line or second-line for advanced/metastatic disease

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Bolus Day 1, then continuous infusion over 2 days, repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Preferred for presumed GI primary with ORR of 50% in retrospective study [5-9].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Bolus Day 1, then continuous infusion over 2 days, repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Preferred for presumed GI primary based on equivalence to CapeOx in colorectal cancer [4,10].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified

drugs.name: Capecitabine
drugs.schedule: Twice daily Days 1–14, repeat every 3 weeks

regimens.notes: Single-agent capecitabine improved relapse-free survival in colon cancer [11].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.notes: Active and well-tolerated with median OS of 16.2 months [12].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified

trial_data.trial_name: Adelaide Cancer Trials and Education Collaborative study

regimens.notes: Response rate of 30.5% with manageable toxicity [13].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.notes: ORR of 41.9% but significant hematologic toxicities [14].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Bolus 1 hour after Leucovorin, repeat every 8 weeks

regimens.notes: Improved DFS and OS in colon cancer adjuvant setting [17].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Bolus Day 1, then continuous infusion over 2 days, repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Standard regimen for GI cancers [18].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Bolus 1 hour after Leucovorin, repeat every week

regimens.notes: Weekly schedule for advanced colorectal cancer [19].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: 24-hour infusion plus Leucovorin 500 mg/m2 Day 1, repeat every week

regimens.notes: Alternative weekly infusion schedule [19].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.notes: Active with ORR of 37.9% and median OS of 16 months [15].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.notes: Active with ORR of 40% and median OS of 10 months [16].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Capecitabine
drugs.schedule: Twice daily Days 1–5, repeat every week for 5 weeks with radiation

regimens.notes: Concurrent chemoradiation for localized disease [20].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Continuous infusion over 24 hours daily Days 1–5, repeat every week for 5 weeks with radiation

regimens.notes: Concurrent chemoradiation for localized disease [21].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.notes: For patients with PS ECOG 0–1. ORR of 18% but higher toxicity than paclitaxel/carboplatin [33].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Bolus Day 1, then continuous infusion over 2 days, repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For PS ECOG 0–1 with presumed GI primary. Significant toxicity but improved outcomes in pancreatic cancer [34].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For patients ineligible for platinum-based chemotherapy
regimens.notes: For patients ineligible for platinum therapy. ORR of 18% and median OS of 8.5 months [35].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified (presumed GI primary)

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Continuous infusion over 2 days, repeat every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For PS ECOG 0–1 with presumed GI primary. Improved DFS and OS in adjuvant pancreatic cancer but high toxicity [36,37].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For advanced/metastatic disease after prior treatment

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must have BRAF V600E mutation

regimens.notes: For BRAF V600E-mutated solid tumors that progressed after prior therapy. ORR of 38% and median OS of 28.6 months [22].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For recurrent or advanced disease after prior treatment

drugs.name: Dostarlimab-gxly
drugs.schedule: Day 1 every 3 weeks for 4 doses, then 1000 mg every 6 weeks

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must be dMMR or MSI-H

regimens.notes: For dMMR solid tumors that progressed on prior therapy. ORR of 42% with median duration of response of 35 months [23].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For advanced/metastatic disease after prior treatment

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must be dMMR or MSI-H

regimens.notes: For dMMR/MSI-H solid tumors. ORR of 34.3% with median OS of 24 months [24-26].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For advanced/metastatic disease after prior treatment
regimens.notes: For TMB-H solid tumors. ORR of 29% with median duration of response not reached [24,25,30].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For metastatic disease after prior treatment

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must have NTRK gene fusion

regimens.notes: For NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors. ORR of 57% with median duration of response of 10 months [27].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For metastatic disease after prior treatment

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must have NTRK gene fusion

regimens.notes: For NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors. ORR of 75% with 71% maintaining response at 1 year [28].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For metastatic disease after prior treatment

drugs.name: Repotrectinib
drugs.schedule: Once daily for 14 days, then twice daily

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must have NTRK gene fusion

regimens.notes: For NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors. ORR of 48% in TKI-pretreated cohort [29].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For advanced/metastatic disease after prior treatment

drugs.name: Selpercatinib
drugs.dose: 120 mg if <50 kg, 160 mg if ≥50 kg

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must have RET gene fusion

regimens.notes: For RET fusion-positive solid tumors. ORR of 44% in evaluable patients [32].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Adenocarcinoma or Carcinoma Not Otherwise Specified
regimens.setting: For advanced/metastatic disease after prior treatment

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must be HER2-positive by IHC 3+

regimens.notes: For HER2-positive solid tumors. ORR of 61.3% and median OS of 21.1 months [31].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Squamous Cell Carcinoma

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Continuous infusion Days 1–5, repeat every 4 weeks

regimens.notes: Historically used for metastatic SCC. Response rate of 54.5% [43].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Squamous Cell Carcinoma
regimens.notes: Active with median OS of 11 months [3].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Squamous Cell Carcinoma
regimens.notes: ORR of 42% with median OS of 11 months [44].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Squamous Cell Carcinoma

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Continuous infusion over 24 hours daily Days 1–4 and 29–32, 35-day cycle with radiation

regimens.notes: Concurrent chemoradiation for esophageal cancer [45].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Squamous Cell Carcinoma

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Continuous infusion over 24 hours daily Days 1–5, 21-day cycle for 2 cycles with radiation

regimens.notes: Alternative concurrent chemoradiation schedule for esophageal cancer [46].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Squamous Cell Carcinoma

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: Continuous infusion Days 1–5, repeat every 3 weeks

regimens.notes: For PS ECOG 0–1. ORR of 80% in head and neck cancer but significant toxicity [47].

regimens.site: Occult Primary, Squamous Cell Carcinoma
regimens.setting: For advanced/metastatic disease after prior treatment

biomarkers.requirement: Tumor must be dMMR/MSI-H or TMB-H (≥10 mut/Mb)

regimens.notes: For dMMR/MSI-H or TMB-H solid tumors [24-26,30].
